name: Arithmetic Coding

x.io: A symbol stream + a probability model → a single fractional number (bitstream) approaching the entropy bound.
preconditions: A probability model of the source (static or adaptive)
Encoder and decoder share the identical model
correctness: Decoder reconstructs symbols exactly given the same model
Approaches Shannon entropy as message length grows
x.stability: Deterministic given the model; bit-exact decode required.
x.parallelisable: Inherently serial within a stream; parallelism via independent blocks.

def: An entropy-coding method that encodes an entire message as a single fractional number in [0,1), achieving compression closer to the entropy limit than Huffman coding.
problem: Squeeze a source toward its theoretical entropy bound, including symbols whose ideal code length is fractional bits.
mechanism: Repeatedly subdivides the current interval in proportion to each symbol’s probability; the final interval identifies the message. Adaptive models update probabilities as symbols are seen. Range coding is an integer-arithmetic variant.
benefits: Near-optimal: handles fractional bits per symbol
Beats Huffman on skewed / adaptive sources
Clean separation of model and coder
limits: Slower and more complex than Huffman
Requires exact-precision arithmetic to avoid drift
failures: Precision / renormalisation bugs desynchronise the stream
whenNot: Throughput-critical paths where Huffman/ANS is fast enough
myths: "Huffman is optimal" — it is optimal only for integer bit lengths; arithmetic coding does better
